advantages: Question-first framing prevents pretty-but-misleading charts and speeds EDA by eliminating library rabbit holes before the goal is clear.
A small set of six chart families covers most ML notebook and reporting needs without chart junk or exotic types.
Explicit variable-type rules transfer across Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly—learn once, implement anywhere.
Pairing histograms with distribution knowledge from Common distributions improves scaler, loss, and outlier decisions.
disadvantages: The six-type framework does not cover every case—network graphs, geographic maps, and Sankey flows need specialized topics.
Box plots summarize away distribution shape; violins or histograms per group may be needed when modality matters.
Heatmaps of large matrices become unreadable without clustering or filtering—overview value drops as dimensionality grows.
Scatter plots break down with extreme overplotting unless paired with density or sampling strategies from implementation topics.
